plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's the complete plan. Ready to implement on your approval:

---

## Plan Summary: JWT Authentication

### 2 New Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/middleware/auth.ts` | `authenticate` middleware — verifies `Bearer` token, attaches `req.user`, returns 401 on failure |
| `src/routes/auth.ts` | `POST /auth/register` (201 + `{token, user}`) and `POST /auth/login` (200 + `{token}`) |

### 4 Modified Files

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `src/types.ts` | Append `declare global` block extending Express `Request` with `user?: { id, username }` |
| `src/app.ts` | Import + mount `authRouter` at `/auth` |
| `src/routes/posts.ts` | Add `authenticate` to POST/PUT/DELETE only (GETs stay public); `userId` from token on POST; 403 ownership check on PUT/DELETE |
| `src/routes/comments.ts` | Same pattern: `authenticate` on POST/PUT/DELETE; `userId` from token on POST; 403 ownership check on DELETE |

### Critical Design Decisions
- **Per-route middleware** (`router.post("/", authenticate, handler)`) — not `router.use()` — keeps GETs public
- **No `expiresIn`** on `jwt.sign` — tokens don't expire mid-test-run
- **404 before 403** on ownership checks — don't leak resource existence
- **Plaintext passwords** — no bcrypt (not in dependencies, matches existing seed data)
